steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the slope of the line $48x - 6y = -32$. Convert to slope-intercept form $y = mx + b$ and read the coefficient of $x$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
Isolate $y$: divide $-6y = -48x - 32$ by $-6$ to get $y = 8x + \tfrac{16}{3}$. The slope is $8$.
  • Choice A — '-8': wrong sign on the $x$-coefficient. ✗
  • Choice B — '$-\frac{3}{2}$': inverted the coefficients and flipped the sign. ✗
  • Choice C — '$\frac{3}{2}$': inverted the coefficients. ✗
  • Choice D — '8': the coefficient of $x$ after isolating $y$. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
